qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Consider the grammar shown below. This grammar is B. SLR(1) but not LL(1) D. LR(I) but not LALR(1) A. LL(1) C. LALR(1) but not SLR(1)

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Consider the translation scheme shown below. num (num.val) Here num is a token that represents an integer and num.val represents the corresponding integer value. For an input string ` ', this translation scheme will print A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Consider the grammar with the following translation rules and as the start symbol Compute E.value for the root of the parse tree for the expression: # & # & A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Which of the following grammar rules violate the requirements of an operator grammar? are nonterminals, and are terminals.
